content.text: Do seven of the following eleven problems. Do not turn in more than seven problems. You must show your work. Answers with no work and/or no explanations will receive no credit. State clearly any theorem you use in your proofs. In the problems, \(\mathbb{Z}\), resp. \(\mathbb{Q}\), \(\mathbb{C}\), is the set of all integers, resp. of all rational numbers, of all complex numbers.

body.text: Consider the following statement \(A(P)\): “If a normal subgroup \(H\) of a group \(G\) and the quotient \(G/H\) both have property \(P\), then so does \(G\).” Prove or disprove \(A(P)\), where

Inside the symmetric group \(S_7\), consider the subgroup \(G\) generated by the permutations \((1234567)\) and \((235)(476)\). Let
\[
H=\langle a,b\mid a^7=b^3=1,\ bab^{-1}=a^2\rangle.
\]
Show that \(G\simeq H\).
Let \(G\) be a finite group with exactly one maximal subgroup. Prove that \(G\) is cyclic of prime power order.
Let \(F\) be a finite field of finite cardinality \(q\) and \(n\) be any natural number. Show that there is at least one irreducible polynomial \(f\in F[x]\) of degree \(n\).
Let \(A\) be a commutative ring with identity. Suppose that \(M\) and \(N\) are free \(A\)-modules with \(m\) and \(n\) generators, respectively. Prove that if \(M\simeq N\), then \(m=n\).

subparts.label: a)
subparts.text: Give an example of a projective \(\mathbb{Z}\)-module and an example of an injective \(\mathbb{Z}\)-module.

subparts.label: b)
subparts.text: Let \(A\) be a finite abelian group considered as a \(\mathbb{Z}\)-module and let \(I\) be an injective \(\mathbb{Z}\)-module. Compute \(A\otimes_{\mathbb{Z}} I\).

body.text: Let \(\alpha\in\mathbb{C}\) be such that \([\mathbb{Q}(\alpha):\mathbb{Q}]=2003\). Let \(E/\mathbb{Q}\) be a normal closure of \(\mathbb{Q}(\alpha)/\mathbb{Q}\). Prove that \([E:\mathbb{Q}]\) divides \(2003!\).
Let \(\xi\in\mathbb{C}\) be a primitive \(77^{\mathrm{th}}\) root of unity in \(\mathbb{C}\), and let \(F=\mathbb{Q}(\xi)\).

subparts.label: a)
subparts.text: Briefly explain why \(F/\mathbb{Q}\) is a Galois extension, and describe the structure of the Galois group \(\operatorname{Aut}_{\mathbb{Q}}(F)\).

subparts.label: b)
subparts.text: Find the number of subfields of \(F\) that are quadratic extensions of \(\mathbb{Q}\).

body.text: State and prove the Hilbert Basis Theorem.
Let \(D\) be a Dedekind domain and \(F\) be the group of fractional ideals of \(D\). Prove that any nontrivial element in \(F\) has infinite order.

Let \(R\) be a commutative ring with identity such that every element \(x\in R\) satisfies \(x^{n(x)}=x\) for some integer \(n(x)\geq 1\) depending on \(x\). Prove that the Jacobson radical of \(R\) is \(0\).
